title: Mutation

records.question: What does mutation explain?
records.answer_summary: Mutation explains the appearance of a new heritable variant. It does not by itself explain the later frequency pattern of that variant.
records.verification_prompt: Separate the first appearance of the variant from the later changes in its frequency.
records.misconception_guard: Do not use mutation as a complete explanation when later mechanisms still need to be named.
records.didactopus_prompt_seed: Write one sentence about origin and one sentence about later frequency change.

records.question: Why can mutation matter even when it is rare?
records.answer_summary: Mutation continually introduces new variation. Over many individuals and many generations, it supplies the raw material that other mechanisms can later change.
records.verification_prompt: Ask whether the explanation is about introducing a new allele or about changing the frequency of an existing one.
records.misconception_guard: Do not confuse rarity of a single event with unimportance over population timescales.
records.didactopus_prompt_seed: Explain why a rare event can still matter across many generations.

records.question: Is every mutation beneficial?
records.answer_summary: No. Mutations can be harmful, neutral, or beneficial depending on their effects and the environment.
records.verification_prompt: Name the trait effect and the environmental context before assigning value.
records.misconception_guard: Do not treat mutation as automatically progressive or helpful.
records.didactopus_prompt_seed: Describe one way the same kind of change could be neutral in one context and costly in another.

records.question: Why distinguish mutation from selection?
records.answer_summary: Mutation introduces a variant; selection can later favor or disfavor it. They are linked, but they are not the same mechanism.
records.verification_prompt: Ask what evidence shows origin and what evidence shows later differential survival or reproduction.
records.misconception_guard: Do not collapse source of variation and filter on variation into one step.
records.didactopus_prompt_seed: Rewrite the explanation so mutation and selection are described as separate stages.

records.question: Why keep drift in the picture after a mutation appears?
records.answer_summary: A new variant can be lost or fixed by chance, especially in small populations, before any selective effect becomes clear.
records.verification_prompt: Compare runs where the new allele appears but no fitness difference is assigned.
records.misconception_guard: Do not assume a new variant persists long enough to matter just because it appeared.
records.didactopus_prompt_seed: Name what evidence would show drift acting on a newly introduced allele.
